notes: Phase TOK follow-up — deterministic adjective→swatch-ID mapping for AIWizard runWizard tunnel handler. When the user's goal text matches keywords below, the harness auto-pins the corresponding swatchID before send() instead of letting the LLM default to retro-neon (the u-028 LLM-soft adjective-mapping miss). Substring matching on the lowercased goal; first match wins (so order matters — put more specific keywords first). To extend: add new keyword to the appropriate swatch list; push to mchatai-source; refreshMchataisourceCache picks it up. Wisdom rule u-028 documents the LLM-soft side; this file is the deterministic guardrail.
_mediaGoalHintsNotes: Phase SWATCH-GUARD (2026-05-21) — when the user's goal contains any of these substrings (lowercased), the harness skips swatch derivation entirely. Rationale: swatches are CSS theming for web-component mini-apps; they have no meaning for image/video/song/speech generation, but goals like 'Generate a watercolor sunset image' were getting wrapped with a 'USE SWATCH sunset-arcade' directive that polluted the user-visible chat log. Tune freely without rebuild — add/remove substrings as the wizard's intent classifier evolves.
steeringTemplate:
=== SWATCH DECISION (NON-NEGOTIABLE) ===
Use swatch ID "{derivedID}" — derived from theme adjectives in the goal.
FORBIDDEN: any swatch other than "{derivedID}".
REQUIRED: applySwatchVariables(document.documentElement, getSwatchByID("{derivedID}"))
REQUIRED: top of CSS :root block must mirror buildSwatchDefaultsCSS("{derivedID}") values verbatim — copy from resources/Swatches.js. The flat color tokens are different per swatch (background/surface/text/accent/accentWarm/stroke/shadow); the structural tokens (radius/space/type/font) are identical across all swatches.

=== USER GOAL ===
{goal}
_steeringTemplateNotes: Prepended to the user goal when deriveSwatchIDFromGoal returns a non-nil match. Placeholders: {derivedID} = matched swatch ID (one of the keys above); {goal} = original user goal text. Tunable from mchatai-source — push + refreshMchataisourceCache + relaunch picks up changes (no Swift rebuild). Empirical note 2026-05-05: even FORBIDDEN/REQUIRED language with prepend placement was ignored by the LLM in TOK.7 canary v2 + v3 — the swatch picked still defaulted to retro-neon despite directive saying 'use sunset-arcade'. Treat this as best-effort LLM-soft guidance; the post-generation patcher (TBD) is the deterministic guarantee. Tune freely without rebuild.
_examples: Goal 'Make me a chess game with a deep purple cinematic theme' → 'deep purple' matches → pin swatchID=sunset-arcade
Goal 'Make me a noir-style mastermind' → 'noir' matches → pin swatchID=vector-noir
Goal 'Make me a synthwave platformer' → 'synthwave' matches → pin swatchID=retro-neon
Goal 'Make me a chess game' → no match → no auto-pin (existing behavior; LLM picks or default applies)
